mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in industrial production and a contraction in real retail sales indicates supply-side bottlenecks outpacing consumer demand, which squeezes corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth by more than 2 standard deviations, or forward returns show no significant difference between high and low interaction states.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising interest rate environment, a monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 3% signals tightening financial conditions that restrict credit availability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline does not precede equity drawdowns when rates are below 1%, or the signal yields a positive IC in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.5% annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% consistently coincides with negative 60-day forward returns, or the predictive IC drops below 0.02 in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 285.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPI breaches the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge in forward return prediction over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 while real retail sales stagnate indicates wage-driven demand without corresponding consumption, creating margin compression risks that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns when PAYEMS growth is decoupled from wage inflation, or forward returns show no significant difference between high and low divergence states.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a simultaneous drop in the unemployment rate signals a labor shortage that prevents firms from scaling back costs, leading to margin squeeze and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns when INDPRO falls and UNRATE drops, or the interaction IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.
